_patch_meta.description: Phase 2 UT review — all 13 untouched verses for H5074 na.dad (mti_id=5571) set aside. All are physical flight, dispersal, or literal animal/bird movement with no inner-being content carried by the term in the verse. OQ-001 (Isa 16:2) resolved as set aside by researcher confirmation.

record.set_aside_reason: na.dad rendered 'thrown away' — describes physical discarding/fate of worthless men. No inner-being state named or engaged by the term in this verse.
na.dad rendered 'heads' — describes the physical gesture of head-wagging by onlookers. No inner-being content at the term level.
na.dad rendered 'flee' — military flight of kings. External behavioural action; no inner-being experience named.
na.dad rendered 'moved' — literal physical movement of birds (none moved a wing). No inner-being content.
na.dad rendered 'flight' — geographic flight of populations before military threat. External physical action; no inner-being state.

na.dad rendered 'fleeing' — functions as vehicle of simile (fleeing birds applied to daughters of Moab). Context carries refugee distress but na.dad operates at the literal-bird end of the simile, not as a direct inner-being term. Set aside per researcher confirmation (OQ-001).
na.dad rendered 'fled' — physical flight from armed threat (swords, bow, press of battle). No inner-being content at the term level.
na.dad rendered 'fled' — military leaders fleeing and captured. External action and outcome; no inner-being content.
na.dad rendered 'flee' — nations flee at divine/military action. Physical dispersal; no inner-being state named.
na.dad rendered 'fled' — literal flight of birds in desolation scene. No inner-being content.
na.dad rendered 'fled' — literal flight of birds and beasts in lament over desolation. No inner-being content.
na.dad rendered 'fugitives' — describes external condition of displaced persons under divine judgment. Inner-being experience not carried by na.dad at the term level in this verse.
na.dad rendered 'fly away' — simile of locusts disappearing in sunlight applied to princes. Physical dispersal/disappearance; no inner-being content.
